metadata.composer: 'Lea' James Lea Summers, (1837-1881) "The Evangelical Lutheran Hymnal" (Ohio Synod), 1908
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Isaac Watts, 1719.  Music: 'Lea' James Lea Summers, (1837-1881).
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.

1.raw_text: Let children hear the mighty deeds Which God performed of old; Which in our younger years we saw, And which our fathers told.

2.raw_text: He bids us make His glories known, His works of pow'r and grace; And we'll convey His wonders down Through every rising race.

3.raw_text: Our lips shall tell them to our sons, And they again to theirs; That genera- tions yet unborn May teach them to their heirs.

4.raw_text: Thus shall they learn in God alone Their hope securely stands, That they may ne'er forget His work But practice His commands.
